data.title: AZZ Inc. Announces Corporate Governance and Other Board Succession Changes
data.description:
AZZ Inc. Announces Corporate Governance and Other Board Succession Changes

AZZ Inc. Announces Corporate Governance and Other Board Succession Changes

PR Newswire

FORT WORTH, Texas, March 5, 2026

FORT WORTH, Texas, March 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- AZZ Inc. (NYSE: AZZ), the leading independent provider of hot-dip galvanizing and coil coating solutions, today announced the appointment of Aaron Schapper, age 52, and Charles "Chuck" Treadway, age 60, to serve as new members of AZZ's Board of Directors, effective April 8, 2026 which is AZZ's first Board meeting of fiscal year 2027.

Mr. Schapper currently serves as President, Chief Executive Officer and a member of the Board of Directors of Myers Industries Inc., a manufacturer of sustainable plastic and metal products that protect the world from the ground up for consumer, vehicle, food & beverage, industrial, infrastructure, and automotive end markets. Prior to joining Myers Industries, Mr. Schapper served in various executive level leadership roles with Valmont Industries, Inc., including Group President, Agriculture and Chief Strategy Officer; Group President of Infrastructure; Group President of Utility Support Structures; Vice President and General Manager, International Irrigation and Global Engineering. Prior to joining Valmont Industries, Mr. Schapper served as General Manager, Asia for Orbit Irrigation Products LLC. Mr. Schapper holds a B.S. in Mechanical Engineering and a B.A. in Mandarin Chinese from the University of Utah. He also holds an Executive M.B.A. from Northwestern University's Kellogg School of Management at Hong Kong University of Science and Technology.

Mr. Treadway currently serves as President, Chief Executive Officer and a member of the Board of Directors of Vistance Networks, Inc., formerly known as CommScope Holding Company, Inc., a global provider of intelligent network solutions for access networks and purpose-driven enterprise networks. Prior to joining Vistance, Mr. Treadway served as Chief Executive Officer of Accudyne Industries. Prior to Accudyne Industries, Mr. Treadway held a number of executive level leadership positions with Thomas & Betts Corporation, including President and Chief Executive Officer, President and Chief Operating Officer, and Group President of Electrical. Mr. Treadway has also served in various senior leadership roles with ABB, Schneider Electric S.A., Prettl International and Yale Security. Mr. Treadway holds a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Louisiana, a M.S. in Electrical Engineering from Clemson University and an M.B.A. from Harvard University.

Additionally, on February 28, 2026, Mr. Dan Feehan announced his retirement from his position as Chairman of AZZ's Board of Directors and was succeeded by Mr. Dan Berce. Mr. Feehan will continue to serve as a Board member of AZZ and as a member of the Compensation Committee and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee until the expiration of his current term in July 2026.

With the appointments of Messrs. Schapper and Treadway, and Mr. Feehan announcing his retirement at the end of his term in July 2026, the Company's Board will be comprised of eight members, with seven being independent, and four being added to the Board within the last five years.

Mr. Berce commented, "Dan has served the shareholders of AZZ with honor and distinction throughout his 26-year tenure on the Board, including seven years as Chairman. We thank him for his exemplary Board leadership and dedicated service to the Company and its shareholders." 

Mr. Berce further noted, "We are pleased Aaron and Chuck have agreed to join AZZ's Board of Directors, both candidates bring significant experience and diverse backgrounds that will add additional depth and diversity to the Board's broad scope of professional expertise, including strategic revenue growth, M&A and ESG. Their appointments represent another meaningful step in our ongoing board refreshment initiatives. These two appointments were the result of a comprehensive search conducted by the Board, with the assistance of a leading independent search firm, as part of the Board's commitment to regular and smooth board succession planning."

About AZZ Inc.

AZZ Inc. is the leading independent provider of hot-dip galvanizing and coil coating solutions to a broad range of end-markets. Collectively, our business segments provide sustainable, unmatched metal coating solutions that enhance the longevity and appearance of buildings, products and infrastructure that are essential to everyday life.

Safe Harbor Statement

Certain statements herein about our expectations of future events or results constitute forward-looking statements for purposes of the safe harbor provisions of The Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. You can identify forward-looking statements by terminology such as "may," "could," "should," "expects," "plans," "will," "might," "would," "projects," "currently," "intends," "outlook," "forecasts," "targets," "anticipates," "believes," "estimates," "predicts," "potential," "continue," or the negative of these terms or other comparable terminology. Such forward-looking statements are based on currently available competitive, financial, and economic data and management's views and assumptions regarding future events. Such forward-looking statements are inherently uncertain, and investors must recognize that actual results may differ from those expressed or implied in the forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements speak only as of the date they are made and are subject to risks that could cause them to differ materially from actual results. Certain factors could affect the outcome of the matters described herein. This press release may contain forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties including, but not limited to, changes in customer demand for our manufactured solutions, including demand by the construction markets, the industrial markets, and the metal coatings markets. We could also experience additional increases in production costs,, due to inflation, in labor costs, components and raw materials including zinc and natural gas, which are used in our hot-dip galvanizing process and the paint used in our coil coating process; customer requested delays of our manufactured solutions; delays in additional acquisition opportunities; an increase in our debt leverage and/or interest rates on our debt, of which a significant portion is tied to variable interest rates; availability of experienced management and employees to implement AZZ's growth strategy; a downturn in market conditions in any industry relating to the manufactured solutions that we provide; economic volatility, including a prolonged economic downturn or macroeconomic conditions such as more inflation or changes in the political stability in the United States and other foreign markets in which we operate; tariffs, acts of war or terrorism inside the United States or abroad; and other changes in economic and financial conditions. AZZ has provided additional information regarding risks associated with the business, including in Part I, Item 1A. Risk Factors, in AZZ's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended February 28, 2025, and other filings with the SEC, available for viewing on AZZ's website at www.azz.com and on the SEC's website at www.sec.gov. You are urged to consider these factors carefully when evaluating the forward-looking statements herein and are cautioned not to place undue reliance on such forward-looking statements, which are qualified in their entirety by this cautionary statement. These statements are based on information as of the date hereof and AZZ assumes no obligation to update any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events, or otherwise.

data.title: My rude, ungrateful relative gave her lawyer power of attorney - and has lived to regret it. Should I get involved?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'A person who is used to controlling others does not like being controlled'

"Don't complain to a lawyer to whom you've given full POA. You may not like the outcome." (Photo subject is a model.)

Dear Quentin,

In a previous letter, I detailed how a relative gave their lawyer full financial and healthcare power of attorney. After my relative was declared mentally incompetent by two doctors, the lawyer activated the POA and is now preparing to place my relative in assisted living, which they do not want. They have now asked me to "fix this."

I obtained some bank statements, and it appears the lawyer added his name to the accounts last July. My relative removed me from the POA and as the beneficiary of their IRA nine years ago. Instead of being appreciative when I said I didn't care about the IRA and would help them anyway, this person became annoyed.

At the time, I had a business to run and employees to manage, but my relative couldn't understand why I wanted to wait until my days off to take care of nonurgent tasks, like changing a light bulb. That's the type of person this is. We had no contact for a long time, but eventually this relative began calling me again with small requests.

A month before the competency exams, this relative asked me to take them to their doctor for "memory issues." Yes, they were forgetting some things, but it seemed normal, and otherwise they appeared fine. I said, "If you want to go to the doctor, I'll take you." I was asked to wait outside during the appointment. The doctor said they were fine.

The path of least resistance

This relative is the type of person who becomes angry if they don't get what they want when they want it. That's why we had a falling out nine years ago. As we were driving home, I said, "Isn't it great? The doctor said you're OK." The response was, "No, it's not great. That's not what I wanted." I suggested getting a second opinion, but I was never contacted about that.

The path of least resistance, I believe, is often what a doctor takes when giving an opinion. "You want us to do something? Fine, we can run more detailed tests and declare you mentally incompetent so your lawyer can take care of you. Won't that be great?" I'm sure my relative liked that idea at the time because they were getting what they wanted.

But now the lawyer is making decisions without them and maintaining control. A person who is used to controlling others does not like being controlled. The bank won't do anything without the lawyer's approval, and they don't like that either. After reflecting on everything that happened - the control, the manipulation - I have washed my hands of this situation.

I don't see the lawyer doing anything wrong other than fulfilling his fiduciary duty as required by state law, based on my research. This is simply a warning and a word to the wise: If you complain about having cognitive issues when your doctor won't give you the attention you seek, don't complain to a lawyer to whom you've given full power of attorney. You may not like the outcome.

Done & Dusted

Related: My dad left when I was 9, reconnected with me in my 50s and now needs money. What do I owe him?

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com. The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

Serving as an authorized agent or co-signer differs greatly from becoming a joint owner of the account and its funds.

Dear Done,

You say your ailing, aging relative is no longer your problem and that they have acted impulsively and shown little gratitude for your past help. At times, they have even been downright rude. That may be true, but I doubt you would have written this second lengthy letter if you did not care at all about this person.

If a court has deemed your relative unable to sign legal documents - and, critically, they granted their lawyer power of attorney while they still had testamentary capacity - then that lawyer will continue making decisions unless a family member petitions the court to appoint someone else or to remove the lawyer from that role. This is a significant decision and not one to take lightly.

A lawyer who holds durable power of attorney, which remains in effect after the principal becomes incapacitated, may generally access the client's bank account. However, access does not equal ownership. Serving as an authorized agent or co-signer differs greatly from becoming a joint owner of the account and its funds.

Attorneys should avoid adding their names to a client's bank account unless a longstanding personal relationship clearly justifies it. If a lawyer tries to create or use a power of attorney against a client's wishes, that conduct could constitute fraud, undue influence, elder abuse or professional misconduct and may lead to civil or even criminal charges.

Likewise, a lawyer may not add themself to a client's account without the client's consent. Attorneys have a fiduciary duty to put their clients' interests first. They may not backdate documents, pressure cognitively impaired clients, transfer funds to their own accounts or add themselves as joint owners without clear authorization.ves as a joint account owner without consent.

Fiduciary duty and familial concern

If you want to help your relative, start by preserving key bank statements, power-of-attorney documents and correspondence from their doctor. Make sure any healthcare directive complies with local medical-consent laws. If a lawyer adds himself to a bank account while holding power of attorney, that could create a serious conflict of interest.

Dementia can cause many symptoms, including physical and verbal aggression and a short temper, especially as the condition worsens. A person may also become extremely confused and paranoid, easily upset, moody and irritable. The disease affects the part of the brain that regulates emotions, and people often feel deep frustration about their declining abilities.

This is a lengthy exchange - two letters and two responses - about someone you claim not to care much about. Perhaps the situation weighs on your conscience more than you admit. Or maybe you worry that if the attorney exploits your relative and leaves them destitute, the rightful heirs will inherit little.

Elder financial abuse is wrong, no matter how difficult the principal may be. They deserve the same protection as someone who inspires you with kindness. Determine whether the attorney had the legal authority to add himself to the accounts. If necessary, report the matter to your state's adult protective services agency and the state bar association.

If you feel compelled to act, you could petition the court to appoint a guardian or conservator to manage your relative's finances and living arrangements and to ensure their comfort for the rest of their life. Your relative may be controlling or manipulative, but do they deserve to be placed somewhere against their wishes?

A friend or family member might serve more compassionately as conservator.

Related: 'It's an expensive piece of equipment': My neighbor asked to borrow my snowblower. Do I say yes?

More columns from Quentin Fottrell:

'She spent $1,000 a month on weight-loss drugs': My son wrecked his finances after meeting his girlfriend. Who's to blame?

Can I stop my kids from using their inheritance to support political causes I vehemently oppose?

My wife's credit-card payment is three months overdue. As an authorized user, am I in trouble?

Check out The Moneyist's private Facebook group, where members help answer life's thorniest money issues. Post your questions, or weigh in on the latest Moneyist columns.

By emailing your questions to The Moneyist or posting your dilemmas on The Moneyist Facebook group, you agree to have them published anonymously on MarketWatch.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms, including via third parties.

-Quentin Fottrell

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  03-05-26 0631ET

data.title: North American Morning Briefing: Stocks Slip Premarket, Oil Climbs Again -2-
data.description:
AUSTIN, Texas-The day after a vicious Republican primary for the U.S. Senate ended in a draw and Democrats picked the candidate most feared by the GOP, President Trump had a message for the candidates headed to a runoff.

"IT MUST STOP NOW!" Trump wrote on Truth Social of the battle between Sen. John Cornyn and state Attorney General Ken Paxton. "I will be making my Endorsement soon, and will be asking the candidate that I don't Endorse to immediately DROP OUT OF THE RACE! Is that fair? We must win in November!!!"

data.title: Trial Set in NEC Multidistrict Litigation Against Reckitt Benckiser’s Mead Johnson Led by Levin Papantonio
data.description:
Trial Set in NEC Multidistrict Litigation Against Reckitt Benckiser’s Mead Johnson Led by Levin Papantonio

Reckitt Benckiser (LON: RKT) subsidiary Mead Johnson is facing a bellwether trial in the US Multidistrict Litigation (MDL) over infant formula product Enfamil linked to necrotizing enterocolitis (NEC) scheduled for 6 July 2026. NEC is a severe intestinal disease that can cause tissue death and life-threatening infections in premature babies. Levin Papantonio attorney Andy Childers is Plaintiffs’ Co-Lead Counsel in the MDL.

The litigation centres on allegations that Mead Johnson’s Enfamil and Abbott Laboratories’ (NYSE: ABT) Similac, which are cow’s-milk-based infant formulas, increase the risk of NEC in premature infants and that manufacturers failed to adequately warn doctors and parents about this risk.

  • More than 775 lawsuits are currently consolidated in the federal MDL in the Northern District of Illinois and hundreds of cases have been filed in state court.
  • Analysts have estimated the total potential liability from infant formula litigation could range from around £400 million to as much as £8 billion.
  • Juries have already returned major verdicts in NEC cases, including $495 million against Abbott Laboratories over Similac and $60 million against Mead Johnson over Enfamil, highlighting the potential financial liability facing formula manufacturers.
  • The state court litigation is preparing for a multi-plaintiff trial against Abbott Laboratories in March.
  • Reckitt Benckiser and Abbott Laboratories have a market share of about 80% of the US infant formula market.

The litigation has become a growing issue as Reckitt Benckiser is exploring the sale of its Mead Johnson nutrition division.

Sara Papantonio, Attorney at Levin Papantonio, said: “The upcoming bellwether trial against Reckitt Benckiser will bring families a step closer to justice after years of corporate misconduct that continues to devastate parents and take the lives of premature babies. For decades, manufacturers of cow’s-milk-based formulas have been aware of the serious risks that these products pose to vulnerable infants, yet they failed to adequately warn doctors and parents. This trial will give victims the opportunity to hold Reckitt Benckiser accountable and to ensure that the full extent of the harm caused by these products is brought before a jury.”

Notes to editors

About Levin Papantonio

Levin Papantonio is one of the leading plaintiff law firms in America. The firm has successfully represented more than 100,000 clients harmed by corporate negligence and misconduct and has helped to secure more than $80 billion in jury verdicts and settlements against some of the world's largest corporations, including Big Tobacco, BP, Dupont, 3M and Merck:

  • Over $14 billion in settlements in the PFAS litigation from 3M, DuPont, Chemours, and Corteva over the last 20 years. These settlements aimed to fund the cleanup of toxic "forever chemicals" from public water systems nationwide, addressing environmental and health concerns linked to PFAS contamination.
  • $500 million settlement with Walgreens, resolving claims that the pharmacy giant recklessly dispensed opioids in 2023.
  • $26 billion national opioid settlement involving Johnson & Johnson and three other major drug distributors in 2021.
  • $18.7 billion settlement in the BP Deepwater Horizon oil spill case in 2010.
  • $4.85 billion settlement in the Vioxx products multi-district litigation against Merck in 2007.
  • $3.75 billion settlement with American Home Products Corporation for those claiming injuries from the diet pill combo Fen-Phen in 1999.
  • $13.2 billion settlement for the State of Florida against the tobacco industry in 1992, the largest civil settlement in US history at the time.

The firm is led by Mike Papantonio, a nationally renowned US trial lawyer. Mike was selected as lead in discovery and trial in the National Prescription Opiate Litigation MDL, dubbed the most complex case in U.S. legal history, and he secured a $26 billion settlement.

One of the few living attorneys inducted into the Trial Lawyer Hall of Fame, Papantonio’s work has been featured in award-winning documentaries including Oxy Kingpins, The Devil We Know, and Jesus Camp. Papantonio also hosts America’s Lawyer on YouTube, with over 1 million subscribers, and he founded and hosts the Ring of Fire radio show where he has interviewed major cultural and political figures, including Al Pacino, Erin Brockovich, and Howard Zinn.

data.title: EPAM Enters Into $300 Million Accelerated Share Repurchase Agreement
data.description:
EPAM Enters Into $300 Million Accelerated Share Repurchase Agreement

EPAM Enters Into $300 Million Accelerated Share Repurchase Agreement

PR Newswire

NEWTOWN, Pa., March 5, 2026

NEWTOWN, Pa., March 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- EPAM Systems, Inc. (NYSE: EPAM), a leading digital and AI transformation company, today announced that it has entered into an accelerated share repurchase agreement (ASR) with Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC to repurchase an aggregate of $300 million of its common stock. The ASR was authorized under the Company's existing $1.0 billion share repurchase authorization.

"Our accelerated repurchase program demonstrates our confidence in EPAM's long-term outlook and our ability to generate sustained growth through our robust engineering foundation and AI-native market leadership," said Balazs Fejes, CEO & President of EPAM. "We believe our current valuation does not fully reflect the strength and future potential of our business and view this action as a value-enhancing use of capital."

Under the terms of the ASR, the Company will make a payment of $300 million to Morgan Stanley and receive from Morgan Stanley an initial share delivery of 1,703,336 shares, or $240 million worth based on the closing price on March 4, 2026. The final number of shares of the Company's common stock to be repurchased under the ASR will be determined on completion of the ASR no later than the second quarter of 2026 and will generally be based on the volume-weighted average share price of the Company's common stock during the term of the ASR, less a discount, and subject to adjustments pursuant to the terms and conditions of the ASR. 

After giving effect to this ASR, the Company has $452.5 million of remaining availability under its current share repurchase authorization.

About EPAM Systems

EPAM (NYSE: EPAM) is a global leader in AI transformation engineering and integrated consulting, serving Forbes Global 2000 companies and ambitious startups. With over thirty years of expertise in custom software, product and platform engineering, EPAM empowers organizations to become AI-native enterprises, driving measurable value from innovation and digital investments. Recognized by industry benchmarks and leading analysts as a leader in AI, EPAM delivers globally while engaging locally, making the future real for clients, partners and employees.

We are proud to be recognized by Forbes, Glassdoor, Newsweek, Time Magazine, Great Place to Work and kununu as a Most Loved Workplace around the world.

Learn more at www.epam.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release includes estimates and statements which may constitute forward-looking statements made pursuant to the safe harbor provisions of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, the accuracy of which are necessarily subject to risks, uncertainties, and assumptions as to future events that may not prove to be accurate. Our estimates and forward-looking statements are mainly based on our current expectations and estimates of future events and trends, which affect or may affect our business and operations. These statements may include words such as "may," "will," "should," "believe," "expect," "anticipate," "intend," "plan," "estimate" or similar expressions. Those future events and trends may relate to, among other things, developments relating to the war in Ukraine and escalation of the war in the surrounding region, political and civil unrest or military action in the geographies where we conduct business and operate, difficult conditions in global capital markets, foreign exchange markets, global trade and the broader economy, the adoption and implementation of artificial intelligence technologies by EPAM and its clients, and the effect that these events may have on client demand and our revenues, operations, access to capital, and profitability. Other factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied include general economic conditions, the risk factors discussed in the Company's most recent Annual Report on Form 10-K and the factors discussed in the Company's Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, particularly under the headings "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations" and "Risk Factors" and other filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Although we believe that these estimates and forward-looking statements are based upon reasonable assumptions, they are subject to several risks and uncertainties and are made based on information currently available to us. data.title: Best Collagen for Women (2026): California Gold Nutrition CollagenUP Named Leading Formula for Skin and Joint Support by Consumer365
data.description:
Best Collagen for Women (2026): California Gold Nutrition CollagenUP Named Leading Formula for Skin and Joint Support by Consumer365

Best Collagen for Women (2026): California Gold Nutrition CollagenUP Named Leading Formula for Skin and Joint Support by Consumer365

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, March 5, 2026

NEW YORK, March 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Consumer365 has recognized California Gold Nutrition CollagenUP as a leading formula for skin and joint support in its 2026 coverage of the best collagen for women. The recognition highlights growing interest in collagen supplementation among women seeking support for skin elasticity, joint comfort, and structural health.

Best Collagen for Women

  • California Gold Nutrition CollagenUP - known for its marine sourced hydrolyzed collagen peptides combined with hyaluronic acid and vitamin C, and is widely available through iHerb.

Collagen has become one of the most discussed supplements in women's health, but its relevance extends beyond beauty trends. Collagen is the body's primary structural protein. It forms the framework of skin, cartilage, bones, tendons, and connective tissue. Natural collagen production begins to decline in the late twenties and decreases more significantly during perimenopause and menopause as estrogen levels change. This decline is associated with visible skin aging, joint stiffness, and reduced bone density.

As awareness of these structural changes grows, more women are exploring collagen supplementation as part of a long term health strategy. The focus has shifted from short term cosmetic improvements to broader structural support.

Why Collagen Matters for Women

Collagen supports skin firmness and elasticity by reinforcing the dermal matrix. As collagen levels decrease, fine lines and reduced skin resilience become more noticeable. Supplementation with hydrolyzed collagen peptides may help provide amino acids that contribute to maintaining skin structure.

Joint health is another key area of concern. Cartilage, which cushions joints, is rich in collagen. Over time, collagen loss can affect joint mobility and comfort. Providing the body with collagen peptides may help supply the building blocks needed for connective tissue repair and maintenance.

Bone health is also linked to collagen. While calcium often receives attention, collagen makes up a significant portion of bone structure. Supporting collagen intake may contribute to overall bone integrity, particularly in women experiencing hormonal shifts.

Collagen's popularity stems from this multi system relevance. It is not limited to one outcome. It plays a foundational role in skin, joints, and bones.

Understanding Collagen Formulation

Not all collagen supplements are formulated in the same way. Hydrolyzed collagen, often referred to as collagen peptides, is the most commonly used form in dietary supplements. Through hydrolysis, collagen is broken down into smaller peptide chains to support absorption and utilization.

Source also matters. Marine collagen, derived from fish, is typically rich in Type I collagen, the predominant type found in skin. Some formulas include Type III collagen, which supports connective tissue and structural integrity.

Effective collagen supplements often include supportive nutrients. Vitamin C is essential for collagen synthesis. Without adequate vitamin C, the body cannot efficiently produce or stabilize collagen fibers. Hyaluronic acid is valued for its role in moisture retention and joint lubrication, contributing to skin hydration and joint comfort.

Consumers increasingly look for formulas that combine these elements rather than relying on collagen alone.

California Gold Nutrition CollagenUP: A Structured Approach

California Gold Nutrition CollagenUP combines hydrolyzed marine collagen peptides providing Types I and III collagen with vitamin C and hyaluronic acid in a single powdered formula. The inclusion of these ingredients reflects a focus on both structural support and hydration.

The collagen is enzymatically processed into low molecular weight peptides to support absorption. Vitamin C is included to assist the body's natural collagen production and to provide antioxidant support. Hyaluronic acid contributes to moisture retention in skin tissue and lubrication within joints.

The formula is manufactured in a cGMP compliant facility and undergoes third party testing for quality standards. Transparency and quality assurance have become important considerations for consumers navigating the supplement market.

The powder format allows flexible dosing and easy incorporation into daily routines. It dissolves in liquids and is designed to integrate into existing wellness habits. For women seeking comprehensive support for skin elasticity and joint mobility, this format aligns with practical, long term use.

Availability through iHerb contributes to consistent access and distribution. As a house brand, California Gold Nutrition maintains visibility within the platform's supplement category, where consumers often research and compare collagen products.

Interest in the best collagen for women continues to expand as more women seek products that address both appearance and structural function. Rather than focusing solely on surface level outcomes, collagen supplementation is increasingly viewed within the context of connective tissue health, mobility, and aging well.

California Gold Nutrition CollagenUP reflects this broader approach. By combining marine collagen peptides with vitamin C and hyaluronic acid, the formula addresses multiple aspects of structural support in one daily serving. Its recognition by Consumer365 highlights ongoing demand for collagen supplements that balance formulation clarity, quality standards, and accessibility.

Readers can access the full review at Consumer365.

About iHerb

iHerb, LLC, is one of the world's leading online retailers dedicated to offering an unbeatable selection of vitamins, minerals, supplements and other health and wellness products, including sports nutrition, beauty, bath and personal care, grocery, baby, and pet care from over 1,800 reputable brands. Supported by a global workforce of 3,000 team members, iHerb serves over 14 million global customers across 180 countries and 36 languages. iHerb's sophisticated global logistics network is anchored by nine climate-controlled logistics centers located in the U.S., Asia, and Saudi Arabia, providing customers with a seamless and reliable shopping experience. Founded in 1996 and based in Irvine, California, iHerb is on a mission to make health and wellness accessible to all. For more information, please visit www.iherb.com.

About Consumer365.org: Consumer365 provides consumer news and industry insights. As an affiliate, Consumer365 may earn commissions from sales generated using links provided.

data.title: Oil prices rise again as conflict in Middle East escalates
data.description:
By Nora Redmond

Oil prices rose after Israel said it had started a "large-scale" attack against Iran.

Oil prices climbed higher on Thursday as investors continued to worry about how the escalating conflict in the Middle East may disrupt global supply chains.

The Brent futures contract (BRN00) , the international benchmark, rose just over 1% to $82.14 a barrel. Prices momentarily jumped to $84.50, approaching close to Tuesday's level, when they reached their highest settlement since January 2025.

West Texas Intermediate contracts (CL00) also temporarily climbed to highs seen previously in the week before beginning to hover at about $76.25 per barrel, a rise of 2% on Wednesday's prices.

Oil futures moved off their highs after Sky News Arabia quoted Iran's deputy foreign minister as saying the country was willing to abandon its nuclear program in response for a lucrative U.S. offer, but it appears the report was referencing his stance at talks in Geneva rather than a new posture since the war began.

The changes also followed Israel announcing on Thursday morning that it had started a "a "large-scale wave of strikes against infrastructure" in Tehran, Iran's capital city. The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has highly disrupted the passage of oil supplies.

A tanker was hit off of Kuwait, resulting in the leaking of oil, according to the U.K. Maritime Trade Operations website, as Iran used a drone to attack Azerbaijan, according to the Associated Press.

Martin Wolf noted in an op-ed published by the Financial Times on Wednesday that neither the change nor level of oil was striking when compared to previous shocks.

Barnard Yaros, lead U.S. economist at Oxford Economics, said: "a $10 increase in oil prices, if sustained, would add 28 cents to the retail price of regular-grade gasoline, subtract 0.1% from real GDP, and keep the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge above 3% in 2026."

"None of these likely negative spillover effects by themselves are enough to derail the current economic expansion," he added.

-Nora Redmond

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  03-05-26 0559ET


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: JD.com Reports First Loss in Over Three Years
data.description:
By Tracy Qu 




  JD.com posted its first quarterly loss in more than three years as the food-delivery subsidy war in China continued to take a toll on the e-commerce giant. 



  The loss underscored the intense pressure the Beijing-based company has come under as it vies for market share in the competitive industry. JD.com has been locked in an intense food-delivery price battle with Meituan and Alibaba Group since entering the market in early 2025, fueling concerns about thin margins and near-term profitability. 



  The online retailer on Thursday swung to a fourth-quarter net loss of 2.71 billion yuan, equivalent to $392.9 million, its first loss since the first quarter of 2022. That compared with net profit of 9.85 billion yuan a year earlier. Analysts had expected a 203.6 million yuan loss, according to a FactSet consensus estimate. 



  Excluding share-based compensation and fair-value changes of long-term investments, among other items, adjusted net profit was to 1.08 billion yuan, down sharply from 11.29 billion yuan the previous year. 



  Revenue for the period rose 1.5% to 352.28 billion yuan, exceeding analysts' expectation of 345.56 billion yuan. 



  The company approved an annual cash dividend of 50 cents a share, representing an aggregate of $1.4 billion to be paid to shareholders. 





  Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  March 05, 2026 05:49 ET (10:49 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Global Procurement Software Market to Reach USD 21.9 Billion by 2035 Amid AI-Driven Digital Transformation
data.description:
Cloud Adoption, ESG Compliance, and Intelligent Automation Propel Market at 9.7% CAGR Through 2035

NEWARK, DELAWARE / ACCESS Newswire / March 5, 2026 / The global procurement software market is entering a high-acceleration growth phase as enterprises worldwide prioritize digital transformation, cost optimization, and supply chain resilience. Valued at USD 7.9 billion in 2025, the market is projected to reach USD 21.9 billion by 2035, expanding at a CAGR of 9.7% during the forecast period.

This robust growth reflects the rising strategic importance of procurement platforms in enabling operational efficiency, regulatory compliance, ESG alignment, and enterprise agility across industries.

According to industry analysts, procurement software is evolving from a transactional back-office system into an intelligent decision-support infrastructure powered by AI, automation, and predictive analytics.

Get Access of Report Sample:
https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/sample/rep-gb-4194

Digital Transformation Reshapes Procurement Operations

The procurement software market experienced strong momentum in 2024, driven by macroeconomic pressures that forced enterprises to enhance spend visibility, automate sourcing, and mitigate supplier risk. Organizations increasingly replaced manual workflows and spreadsheets with integrated cloud-based platforms capable of managing end-to-end procurement lifecycles.

By 2025, mid-sized enterprises are expected to significantly increase adoption, transitioning from legacy systems to scalable SaaS procurement suites. Embedded AI capabilities - including contract intelligence, spend forecasting, and supplier risk scoring - are transforming procurement teams into strategic value creators rather than cost centers.

An industry executive notes:

"Procurement is no longer measured solely by cost savings. It is now evaluated by its ability to deliver resilience, sustainability, and real-time decision intelligence."

Marketplace Innovation: AWS Launch Signals Structural Shift

One of the most impactful developments occurred in May 2025, when Amazon Web Services (AWS) launched AWS Marketplace in India, enabling local currency transactions via UPI, net banking, and credit cards. This initiative has been described as a transformative moment for enterprise software procurement in one of the fastest-growing global markets.

The localization of enterprise procurement - including regional compliance support and payment flexibility - reflects a broader industry trend toward accessible, frictionless software acquisition models.

Technology Evolution: From Automation to Intelligent Procurement Infrastructure

Procurement software platforms are rapidly integrating advanced technologies that redefine operational performance:

  • AI-powered predictive analytics for spend optimization

  • Automated contract lifecycle management

  • Supplier risk scoring and compliance monitoring

  • ESG tracking and sustainability dashboards

  • Blockchain-enabled smart contracts

  • Robotic Process Automation (RPA) for requisition workflows

Modern procurement suites now offer centralized dashboards covering sourcing, supplier onboarding, purchasing, invoicing, and analytics - eliminating fragmented third-party tools.

Cloud-native, API-first architectures are enabling seamless ERP integration and real-time financial synchronization, reinforcing procurement as digital enterprise infrastructure.

Regulatory and Standards Momentum Accelerates Adoption

Governments and international bodies in 2025 advanced digital procurement standards emphasizing AI governance, interoperability, and sustainability reporting.

  • The UK issued structured AI procurement evaluation guidelines

  • India expanded modernization of its Government e-Marketplace

  • Australia streamlined digital sourcing through BuyICT

  • The EU enhanced structured eForms and eProcurement Ontology frameworks

  • The OECD reinforced green public procurement metrics

These initiatives collectively increase demand for compliant, transparent, and policy-aligned procurement platforms.

Segment Spotlight

E-Procurement Solutions Lead Software Type

E-procurement solutions account for 24% market share in 2025. Their strength lies in automating requisition-to-order cycles, enforcing compliance controls, and reducing maverick spending. Integration with supplier portals and spend analytics tools continues to drive enterprise adoption.

Cloud-Based Deployment Dominates

Cloud deployment holds a commanding 68% market share in 2025. Organizations prefer SaaS models due to scalability, rapid implementation, lower capital expenditure, and seamless AI integration.

Cloud-first procurement ecosystems are expected to remain the dominant deployment model through 2035.

Industry-Specific Adoption Trends

  • Retail & E-commerce: Focus on supply chain responsiveness and real-time inventory procurement

  • Manufacturing: ERP integration, supplier collaboration, cost optimization

  • Healthcare: Regulatory compliance and traceability

  • BFSI: Fraud prevention and financial transparency

  • IT & Telecom: Global supplier coordination and license management

AI-driven predictive analytics, blockchain-backed contract automation, and ESG-enabled sourcing remain cross-sector growth drivers.

Risk Landscape

Despite strong growth, the procurement software market faces several risks:

  • Cybersecurity threats targeting financial and supplier data

  • ERP integration complexity and legacy system dependency

  • Regulatory non-compliance across multinational operations

  • Vendor dependency for cloud hosting and system upgrades

  • Budget constraints and ROI justification challenges

Vendors prioritizing robust cybersecurity, modular integration, and compliance automation will mitigate these risks effectively.

Competitive Landscape: AI-Centric Platforms Define Leadership

The procurement software market is highly competitive, with leading vendors offering integrated, AI-driven enterprise suites.

Key Market Players:

  • SAP Ariba

  • Coupa Software

  • Jaggaer

  • GEP Smart

  • Basware

  • Zycus

  • Proactis

  • Workday Procurement

Emerging vendors are disrupting the market with low-code contract management platforms, blockchain-based procurement transparency tools, and AI procurement bots.

Strategic partnerships, M&A activity, and cloud-native expansion remain central competitive strategies.

Future Outlook: Intelligent, ESG-Driven Procurement Ecosystems

Looking toward 2035, key market-shaping trends include:

  • Autonomous AI-driven procurement bots

  • ESG-integrated supplier scoring systems

  • Predictive spend forecasting engines

  • Embedded financing and payment automation

  • Mobile-first procurement platforms

  • Blockchain-enabled smart contract execution

As procurement becomes central to enterprise resilience, sustainability, and risk management, digital procurement platforms will serve as mission-critical infrastructure rather than optional tools.

The global procurement software market is therefore positioned for sustained double-digit expansion in major economies, redefining how enterprises manage sourcing, compliance, and supplier collaboration in the decade ahead.

data.title: /C O R R E C T I O N — basys.ai/
data.description:
Basys.ai CEO Amber Nigam Recognized Among Top Healthcare Technology CEOs of 2025

Basys.ai CEO Amber Nigam Recognized Among Top Healthcare Technology CEOs of 2025

PR Newswire

BOSTON, March 4, 2026

Recognition highlights leadership in building reliable AI systems for regulated healthcare workflows

BOSTON, March 4, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Basys.ai today announced that its CEO and co-founder Amber Nigam has been recognized among the Top Healthcare Technology CEOs of 2025 by The Healthcare Technology Report. The recognition highlights leadership in developing reliable AI systems designed to support complex healthcare operations and regulated clinical workflows.

The full list of recognized leaders can be viewed here.

The recognition comes amid growing attention across the healthcare industry on how artificial intelligence can help address operational complexity in areas such as utilization management, prior authorization, and program integrity.

"I'm honored to receive this recognition on behalf of the team basys", said Amber Nigam, CEO of Basys.ai. "This work reflects the effort of an exceptional team focused on building agentic AI systems that operate reliably in real healthcare environments while maintaining transparency, auditability, and alignment with regulatory requirements."

Basys.ai develops domain-specific AI systems designed to support complex clinical and administrative workflows across healthcare organizations. The company's technology helps healthcare stakeholders interpret policies, analyze medical records, and improve the efficiency of operational processes that traditionally rely on extensive manual review.

The engagement builds on Basys.ai's growing recognition for its work in applying AI to regulated healthcare workflows. The company was recently selected as one of the Top 10 finalists (from 250+ submissions) in CMS's Chili Cook-off on Fraud, Waste, and Abuse, which highlighted innovative approaches to improving program integrity.

"Healthcare is at the precipice of a major technological transformation," Nigam said. "But deploying agentic AI in clinical and administrative workflows requires thoughtful guardrails. Large language models can produce confabulations or hallucinations if left unchecked, which is unacceptable in regulated healthcare environments. That is why we have built a unique moat around our platform through guardrails and validation systems designed to minimize hallucinations and ensure reliable, transparent outcomes."

Nigam, a Harvard alum, has also contributed to broader conversations on responsible AI deployment in healthcare. His perspectives on deploying domain-specific AI systems have been published in Harvard Business Review (HBR), including a recent article discussing how organizations can evaluate generalist versus specialized AI models in enterprise environments:

He has also spoken on aligning incentives across healthcare stakeholders at TEDx, where he discussed structural challenges in healthcare delivery and opportunities for innovation.

In addition, Nigam has been invited to speak on healthcare innovation and data science at The Harvard Chan Studio, hosted by Harvard University, and Stanford University. Nigam's leadership has been acknowledged across the digital health ecosystem. He was recognized by Rock Health as one of the Top 50 in Digital Health, highlighting leaders shaping the future of healthcare innovation.

Nigam was also named among the Boston Business Journal's 2025 "40 Under 40" honorees, recognizing emerging leaders making significant impact across industries.

Basys.ai and Nigam's work has also been spotlighted in several industry forums and publications, including STAT News and research discussions referenced by Gartner, reflecting growing attention to the role of reliable AI systems in regulated healthcare environments.

Basys.ai continues to focus on developing agentic AI systems designed for complex healthcare operations, combining policy interpretation, medical record analysis, and explainable decision workflows while maintaining reliability and compliance with healthcare regulatory frameworks.

Nigam concluded, "We're already seeing the early impact of this approach through basys.ai's work supporting critical programs, including contracts with CMS. Our goal is to deploy AI in ways that reduce administrative burden, flatten the cost curve in healthcare, and promote fairness, accuracy, and transparency in how decisions are made."

About Basys.ai

Basys.ai is a healthcare AI company focused on building domain-specific agentic AI systems for clinical and administrative workflows, including prior authorization, utilization management, and payment integrity. The company develops AI systems designed to support healthcare organizations with transparent, auditable, and operationally reliable decision support.

Learn more:
https://www.basys.ai 

Media Contact:
communications@basys.ai
